data.title: High-Frequency Trader Closes Bitcoin Short Position with Loss
data.description:
On January 23, a high-frequency trader known as 'Pioneer of Short Selling' (0x3bcae) closed a short position of 68.95 BTC, incurring a loss of $14,000. According to BlockBeats, the trader currently holds only short positions with an unrealized loss of $15,000.

The address employs a high-frequency, multi-asset strategy, favoring overnight positions with strict risk management.

data.title: Bitcoin Network Sees Hashrate Increase Amid Rising Prices
data.description: The Bitcoin network's hashrate experienced a 3.4% increase this week, reaching an average of 1039 EH/s. According to NS3.AI, the average Bitcoin price also saw a slight rise of 0.61%, bringing it to approximately $92,872. In a notable development, Ethiopia is considering partnerships to establish Bitcoin mining operations, signaling a significant move in the industry. Furthermore, two independent miners successfully mined a block each, earning rewards of around $300,000.

data.title: Farcaster Protocol to Continue Operations Amid Acquisition
data.description: Farcaster co-founder Dan Romero has confirmed that the Farcaster protocol will continue to operate and will not be shut down. According to NS3.AI, the protocol's acquirer, Merkle, plans to return the $180 million previously raised from investors. Romero also addressed rumors regarding his recent home purchase, providing clarification on the matter.

data.title: This week's top mining news highlights: Ethiopia explores Bitcoin mining strategies; Two independent miners each produce a block and receive approximately $300,000 in rewards.
data.description: Week 4 of 2026 (January 16 - January 22): 1. According to cloverpool, the average hashrate of the Bitcoin network was 1039 EH/s, with a high of 1164 EH/s and a low of 947 EH/s, a 3.4% increase from the previous week's average hashrate (1005 EH/s). 2. According to blockchain.com, the average price of Bitcoin was $92,872, with a high of $95,830 and a low of $87,156, a 0.61% increase from the previous week's average price ($92,312). 3. Mining news worth noting includes: (1) Ethiopia seeks partners to explore Bitcoin mining layout; (2) Two independent miners produced blocks this week and received a reward of about $300,000; (3) NIP Group disclosed that it produced 151.4 BTC in the initial operation period of Bitcoin mining; (4) Bitdeer sold 146.8 BTC this week, and its Bitcoin holdings dropped to about 1,502; Data partner: Cango Inc. (CANG), a Bitcoin mining company listed on the New York Stock Exchange.

data.title: BitMart has listed DisclaimerCoin (DONT) in its "BM Discover" section.
data.description: BitMart's "BM Discover" section launched DisclaimerCoin (DONT) on January 23 at 12:00 (UTC+8), and the DONT/USDT trading pair was opened at this launch.

data.title: Fund managers' cash holdings hit a record low, reflecting extremely bullish investor sentiment.
data.description: Data from BofA's global fund manager survey shows that fund managers' cash holdings have fallen to a record low of 3.2%, reflecting investors' extremely bullish sentiment towards risky assets. (Barchart)

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Discusses Bitcoin's Origins and Principles
data.description: Vitalik Buterin recently shared his thoughts on Bitcoin's inception, emphasizing its foundational mindset. According to Foresight News, Buterin highlighted that Bitcoin was created with a resistance to the impulse of chasing universally popular tools. Instead, it adhered to principles that focus on building and utilizing tools that safeguard user autonomy and freedom.

data.title: Emerging Markets Embrace Stablecoins Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description:
Emerging markets increasingly adopt stablecoins, driven by inflation and cross-border remittance demand, according to S&P Global Ratings.
source: https://coincu.com/news/emerging-markets-stablecoins-adoption/

data.title: Whale Engages in High-Leverage Ethereum Trading
data.description:
On January 23, a whale executed a high-leverage trade involving 4,212 ETH at 11:16, according to BlockBeats. The position was opened with a 25x leverage at an average price of $2,962.62, currently showing a slight unrealized loss.

The address has been actively trading ETH and BTC recently, primarily engaging in short-term, high-leverage isolated positions.

data.title: MilkyWay Concludes Final Revenue Distribution Amid Shutdown
data.description: MilkyWay has completed its last protocol revenue distribution, allocating 92,708 USDC converted from fees in TIA, INIT, and BABY tokens to eligible community members. According to NS3.AI, participants in this distribution included MILK token holders, stakers, and liquidity providers, with a minimum threshold set at 1 USDC. The project is winding down and will permanently shut down due to lower-than-expected DeFi adoption and delayed funding solutions.

data.title: Nasdaq has applied to remove the 25,000-contract position limit for Bitcoin and Ethereum ETF options.
data.description: Nasdaq has filed a rule change application with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), seeking to remove the 25,000-unit position limit for spot Bitcoin and Ethereum ETF options, aligning crypto ETF options with rules for other commodity funds. Nasdaq stated that this move would give crypto ETF options equal treatment with other commodity options, eliminating unequal restrictions. The SEC has opened a comment period, with a final decision expected by the end of February. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: SEC, CFTC to hold harmonization event on crypto oversight
data.description:
The SEC’s Paul Atkins and the CFTC’s Michael Selig will discuss the two agencies' crypto oversight as legislative efforts to define their roles work through the Senate.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/sec-cftc-harmonization-event-crypto-oversight?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Asia Market Open: Bitcoin Dips Below $90K, Wall Street Rebound Lifts Asia Risk Mood
data.description:
Bitcoin slipped below $90,000 as Asian stocks edged higher after the Bank of Japan held rates, with investors weighing softer US tariff talk and firm US data.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/bitcoin-price-wall-street-rebound-lifts-asia-risk-mood-asia-market-open/

data.title: Cosmos Labs Engages in Talks with Circle Regarding Native USDC Issuance
data.description: Cosmos Labs is reportedly in discussions with Circle about issuing a native USDC, according to Foresight News. The information was shared by tøny on Twitter, citing a recent Telegram chat where Cosmos Labs Co-CEO magmar disclosed the ongoing talks between the two teams.

data.title: WLFI advisor ogle went long on ZRO with 5x leverage and even added to his spot position two weeks ago.
data.description: Lookonchain monitoring shows that WLFI advisor ogle (@cryptogle) went long 347,280 ZRO (worth $795,000) 5x in the past 2 hours. Two weeks ago, he also spent $50,000 to buy 33,411 ZRO (currently worth $755,000).

data.title: Makina: The funds held by MEV Builder have been returned and transferred to a dedicated recovery account.
data.description: Makina, the DeFi execution engine that was previously hacked, posted on its official X account that at 21:15 on January 22, MEV Builder returned the funds according to the SEAL white hat safe harbor protocol and deducted 10% of the bounty, returning approximately 920 ETH (out of a total of 1023 ETH), accounting for approximately 1299 ETH of the total stolen funds; the funds have been transferred to the recovery multisignature address 0xc22F…8AB9, and the team is continuing to pursue the remaining funds and seeking to contact the RocketPool validator address 0x573D…910E, which received approximately 276 ETH.

data.title: MilkyWay has completed the final protocol revenue distribution to the community.
data.description: MilkyWay announced the completion of its final protocol revenue distribution to the community, distributing a total of 92,708 USDC. This amount was converted from fees collected in the form of TIA, INIT, and BABY tokens. The distribution was based on a final protocol snapshot, and eligible participants included MILK holders, MILK stakers, and liquidity providers. A minimum threshold of 1 USDC was set for this distribution; addresses with less than 1 USDC were not eligible. Previously, MilkyWay announced it was gradually ceasing operations and would permanently shut down. MilkyWay stated that the demand and adoption of decentralized finance had not met expectations, and the WayCard launch was too late to alleviate funding pressures in a timely manner.

data.title: XRP Spot ETF Sees Significant Inflow on January 22
data.description:
On January 22, XRP spot ETF experienced a net inflow of $2.09 million, according to ChainCatcher. This inflow was solely attributed to the Franklin XRP ETF XRPZ, marking a single-day net inflow of $2.09 million and bringing the historical total net inflow to $293 million.

As of the latest update, the total net asset value of the XRP spot ETF stands at $1.37 billion, with an XRP net asset ratio of 1.17%. The cumulative historical net inflow has reached $1.23 billion.

data.title: Ethereum Mainnet Surpasses Layer 2 Networks in Daily Active Addresses
data.description: Ethereum's mainnet has seen a significant increase in daily active addresses, surpassing all Layer 2 networks. According to ChainCatcher, data from Token Terminal indicates that the number of active addresses on Ethereum's mainnet has exceeded those on Layer 2 solutions. This development highlights Ethereum's continued dominance in the blockchain space, as it maintains a higher level of user engagement compared to its Layer 2 counterparts.
